mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly lower corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 24-month window or positive return frequency drops below 55% during rate cuts.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid three-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.5 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with p > 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous monthly increases in real personal income and housing starts indicate synchronized consumer and construction demand expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform individual series by at least 0.05 in Rank IC or positive return frequency falls below 50% when both series rise together.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that offsets higher financing costs, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during tightening periods or produces negative returns in > 55% of instances when INDPRO increases month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls directly increases aggregate wage income and corporate revenue expectations, yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Sharpe ratio remains below 0.5 over any 24-month window or the signal shows no predictive power with Rank IC below 0.01 across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase exceeding 0.4% triggers expectations of accelerated monetary tightening and margin compression, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in > 65% of triggered instances or the inflation threshold fails to separate winning and losing months with statistical significance.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth deceleration coinciding with rising unemployment indicates a broad economic slowdown that reduces corporate earnings forecasts, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses explanatory power with p > 0.05 in multivariate regression or fails to predict negative returns more often than random chance over a 5-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Declining industrial production during a post-shock recovery phase signals persistent supply chain bottlenecks and weak manufacturing demand, resulting in negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The mechanism produces positive returns in > 60% of triggered instances or the predictive accuracy drops below 52% in out-of-sample walk-forward validation.
